commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the OpenAI Responses transport replay gate to omit prior response item IDs when the resolved payload policy strips `store`, and adds a regression test for storeless custom providers.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +93. Total +94 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main’s replay default ignores `shouldStripStore`, and the PR adds a payload-capture regression for `compat.supportsStore=false`. I did not rerun the tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the PR already contains the narrow code and test change and there are no actionable review findings.

target.title: fix(OpenAI Responses): disable item id replay for storeless providers
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89728.
Refs #90657 as the first, narrow fix lane from the Actions-backed tracking matrix.

Custom OpenAI Responses-compatible providers can have effective Responses `store` support stripped (`compat.supportsStore=false`). In that storeless mode, replaying prior Responses item ids is unsafe because the provider cannot resolve server-stored items from prior turns.

This patch makes the agent Responses builder gate item-id replay on both conditions:

```ts
payloadPolicy.explicitStore !== false && !payloadPolicy.shouldStripStore
```

instead of only checking `explicitStore !== false`.

## Root cause

For storeless custom `openai-responses` providers, the payload policy can resolve to:

```text
explicitStore === undefined
shouldStripStore === true
```

The old replay gate treated this as replay-safe because `undefined !== false` is true. That allowed prior assistant Responses item ids to be serialized even though the effective request cannot rely on server-side stored item lookup.

## Changes

- Disable Responses item-id replay when the resolved payload policy will strip `store`.
- Add a regression test for a custom `openai-responses` model with `compat.supportsStore=false`.
- The test verifies:
  - no replayed `id` is emitted for prior `reasoning`, `message`, or `function_call` items;
  - `function_call` / `function_call_output` `call_id` pairing remains intact;
  - existing OpenAI-native replay behavior remains covered by the existing tests.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Storeless custom `openai-responses` providers must not receive replayed prior Responses item ids. The user-visible bug class is provider-side continuation failure: a custom-compatible provider cannot resolve item ids that were never stored server-side. After this patch, prior `reasoning`, `message`, and `function_call` `id` fields are omitted when policy strips `store`, while tool-call `call_id` pairing is preserved.

Real environment tested: Current PR head `fb69711a5a6d1162a6e51e3f60f1fc4e3d4e9c27` in a Linux OpenClaw checkout. The reproduction uses OpenClaw's real `stream(...)` / `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` path with a deterministic custom `openai-responses` model. Network I/O is intentionally aborted after payload capture; no external provider credentials are required. The related #90657 matrix also ran on GitHub-hosted Linux runners.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts
# 1 file passed, 10 tests passed
# includes: omits Responses item ids for storeless custom providers while preserving tool call ids

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts
# 4 files passed, 269 tests passed

pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.ts
# Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

git diff --check
# passed
```

Additional linked Actions evidence from the #90657 reproduction matrix:

- Fixture/source baseline: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27005369159
- Direct OpenClaw code-path reproduction: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27007044935
- Gap closure retest: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27012993404

Observed result after fix: The storeless custom-provider regression test captures a Responses payload where replayed `id` fields are absent from prior `reasoning`, `message`, and `function_call` items. The `function_call` still has a non-empty `call_id`, and the matching `function_call_output.call_id` equals the `function_call.call_id`. Existing OpenAI-native replay tests continue to pass.

What was not tested: No live custom provider endpoint was called from this PR; the regression aborts before network I/O after capturing the real OpenClaw-built payload. This PR does not fix or claim to fix sibling issues tracked in #90657, including stream `final_answer` normalization, Azure-style empty item type validation, Slack continuation symptoms, Discord duplicate delivery, or #90094 `content:null`. A local graphify rebuild was attempted for the full OpenClaw checkout, but it produced only a partial lock/root marker and no usable `GRAPH_REPORT.md`; the partial output was removed and is not part of this PR.

## Scope / non-goals

This PR intentionally fixes only the #89728 same-root replay gate.

It does not claim to fix the sibling issues tracked in #90657, such as stream `final_answer` normalization, Azure-style empty item type validation, Slack continuation symptoms, Discord duplicate delivery, or #90094 `content:null`.

## Risk

Low, narrow behavior change:

- Native OpenAI-compatible stored Responses replay remains unchanged when the policy allows store-backed replay.
- Explicit `store=false` already disabled replay; this adds the missing store-stripped custom-provider case.
- Tool call continuity remains protected by preserving `call_id` pairing even when item ids are omitted.
